name: Eddie "Rochester" Anderson
abstract: Edmund Lincoln Anderson (September 18, 1905 – February 28, 1977) was an American actor and comedian known for his gravelly voice. To a generation of early radio and television comedy audiences, he was known as "Rochester".

about_quotes.speaker: Leonard Phillips
about_quotes.evidence: I called them all. Jack Holt, Sam Adams, Francis Whitby, Arthur Williams, me, Pete Jones, Eddie Anderson who played bass, then Debo Mills who played the drums, and Charlie Toran on piano.
